content: Psychotherapy is primary treatment. Borderline PD: DBT (strongest evidence), MBT, TFP, schema therapy. Antisocial PD: limited evidence, focus on substance use comorbidity, contingency management. Avoidant/Dependent/OCPD: CBT, schema therapy, psychodynamic approaches. Cluster A: supportive therapy, social skills training; antipsychotics may help cognitive-perceptual symptoms. No medications approved specifically for PDs; pharmacotherapy targets specific symptoms (mood stabilizers for impulsivity/affective instability, SSRIs for anxiety/depression, atypical antipsychotics for cognitive-perceptual symptoms). Avoid polypharmacy. Treatment engagement challenging; therapeutic alliance critical. Long-term perspective needed; personality change gradual. Comorbid conditions (depression, anxiety, SUD) require concurrent treatment.
